intended_use_full: MRCAT imaging is intended to provide the operator with information of tissue properties for radiation attenuation estimation purposes in photon external beam radiotherapy treatment planning. MRCAT Head and Neck is indicated for radiotherapy treatment planning for patients with soft tissue tumors in the Head and Neck region.
target_condition: soft tissue tumors in the head and neck region
ground_truth: registered CT images from several sites used for HU value calibration; CT-based treatment plans used as reference standard for dose accuracy comparison
other_metrics: Dose accuracy: 95% of patients meet gamma analysis criterion 2%/2mm in 98% of voxels within PTV or exceeding 75% of maximum dose; average dose deviation ≤5% or 1 Gy (whichever greater) in 99% of patients in sensitive organs compared to CT-based plan. Geometric accuracy: ±1 mm (200 mm sphere), ±2 mm (400 mm sphere), ±5 mm (500 mm sphere). PTV dose differences <1% with very small bias when using MRCAT instead of CT.
